Lacedaemon in her king ; roam both in her consul and dictator , and Venice in her duke . answer . government whether popular or monarchical be equal artificial ; wherefore to know which be the more natural , we must consider what piece of art come near unto nature ; as for example , whether a ship or a house be the more natural , and then it will be easy to resolve that a ship be the more natural at sea , and a house at land in like manner where one man or a few man be the landlord , monarchy must doubtless be the more natural , and where the whole people be the landlord , a commonwealth ; for how can we understand that it shall be natural unto a people that can live of themselves , to give away the mean of their livelihood to one or a few man that they may serve or obey ? each government be equal artificial in effect or in it self , and equal natural in the cause , or the matter upon which it be found . a commonwealth consist of the senate propose , the people resolve , and the magistracy execute ; so the power of the magistrate ( whether , king as in Lacedaemon , consul as in roam , or duke as in Venice ) be but bare executive , but to a monarch belong both the result and execution too ; wherefore that there have be duke , consul , or king in common wealth , which be quite of another nature , be no argument that monarchical government be for this cause the more natural . and if a man shall instance in a mix government , as king and parliament , to say , that the king in this be more natural than the parliament , must be a strange affirmation . to argue from the roman dictator , a imperfection which ruin that commonwealth and be not to be find in any other , that all commonwealth have have the like recourse in exigence unto the like remedy , be quite contrary to the universal testimony of prudence or story . a man who consider that the commonwealth of Venice have stand one thousand year , which never any monarchy do , and yet shall affirm for in those of force , there be more of the image of the beast ; but in those of the pen , there be more of the image of God . in the controversy of the sword , there be but too often no other reason then force ;
